mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and supports higher equity valuations, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when rate cuts occur during acute recession fears and equities reprice lower despite easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are negative in most periods when FEDFUNDS trends down for 2 consecutive months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above the recent trend indicates slowing household income and weaker earnings expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises alongside strong labor-demand revisions that keep risk assets bid.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show materially lower 20d SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if the sign of the effect turns positive for a full 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, nominal spending and payroll income are expanding at the same time, which supports near-term revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days. The effect fails if sales growth is broad but payroll growth is absent, because then demand is not translating into income.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform each series alone in predicting 20d SPX returns, or if the combined signal loses positive out-of-sample Rank IC in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior slowdown indicates supply-side normalization and improving earnings capacity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if industrial output rises only because of inventory rebuilding without subsequent margin improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are not positive on average following upward INDPRO momentum, or if the effect disappears once restricted to periods with flat-to-falling CPI.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST below its recent baseline indicates housing-demand contraction and a weaker cyclical outlook, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when housing weakness is offset by strong rate relief and equity multiples expand instead.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered declines in HOUST are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal’s conditional hit rate is indistinguishable from 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, real-rate pressure remains restrictive and equity duration should reprice lower, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if inflation is accelerating but the market is already pricing rapid cuts and stocks rally on easing expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-plus-policy state does not produce lower 5d returns than either proxy alone, or if 5d returns are positive in a majority of high-inflation restrictive episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE with expanding PAYEMS indicates a still-tight labor market and income growth that supports risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when employment growth is concentrated in low-quality hiring and does not translate into consumption.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays below 4.8% while PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered joint state fails to outperform the unconditional baseline over 60d horizons, or if rolling 3-year directional accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 trend confirms aggregate real demand expansion and supports higher corporate revenues, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when GDP growth is driven by transitory inventory effects rather than final demand.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are not positive following rising GDPC1 growth, or if the effect vanishes after excluding quarters with weak RSAFS growth.
